biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — e7c8f9ae-2: lsof+wc-l+head via Bash (should use dedicated tools); e7c8f9ae-3: lsof via Bash; bc2715fa-2,5,7,14,22,29,30,32 repeat historical pattern; new session rate ~2/8 units (25%) is lower than historical bc2715fa 62% — possible improvement but sample too small to confirm trend

hook_bypass_via_absolute_grep_path:RECURRING:IMPROVING — ZERO instances of /usr/bin/grep in e7c8f9ae session (8 units); bc2715fa-21,22,29,30 carry historical rate; first new session in measured history with zero absolute-grep calls; do not relax — confirm over next 2+ sessions before reclassifying as resolved

optimistic_write_without_read:RECURRING:STABLE — e7c8f9ae-16: Write /Users/alcatraz627/.claude/_last-checkpoint.json success=false x1 before Read pivot then Write success=true; identical topology to all prior instances; write-tool contract (Read required before overwriting existing file) violated again; pattern has appeared in every session with checkpoint writes

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:STABLE — e7c8f9ae-2: Edit dashboard.js success=false before Read; bc2715fa-14,22,32 carry historical instances; new session contributes 1 instance; pattern of attempting edits without prior Read on the specific target file persists across sessions

declared_ready_without_runtime_exercise:RECURRING:CONFIRMED — bc2715fa-14 violates; bc2715fa-18 correctly self-documents; not observed in e7c8f9ae units measured here; ritual of self-documentation executes correctly but does not prevent violation in subsequent cycles
trusted_linter_reminder_without_diffing:RECURRING:CONFIRMED — bc2715fa-22 carries the instance; not observed in e7c8f9ae units; eighth consecutive cycle carrying this pattern in history

ask_user_question_instead_of_inputs_mcp:NEW — e7c8f9ae-8: AskUserQuestion used to select Render workspace; CLAUDE.md MCP tool preferences (mandatory) specify mcp__inputs__confirm/pick_one over AskUserQuestion for structured input; first observed instance; not previously flagged

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:IMPROVING — ZERO instances in e7c8f9ae session; bc2715fa-5 and bc2715fa-7 carry historical pattern; new session units show correct path handling without cd-prefix failures or wrong-path rg targeting; second consecutive new-session observation with zero instances

cross_references.note: Write _last-checkpoint.json without prior Read is a direct instance — asserting the file does not exist or can be overwritten without observing its current state. Dream insight violation confirmed.
Edit dashboard.js without prior Read is same class of assertion failure — assuming old_string is present without verifying. Violation confirmed.

Highest re_edit_thrash concentration in this batch (Edit with-server.ts x3+). Unit-14 is a long complex unit spanning multiple files — consistent with insight that large complex multi-file units produce more fix-thrash cycles.

Token extracted from log output (rg -o on /tmp/lc-test.log) rather than from a config file. Log extraction is contextually reasonable for a test token generated at runtime, not a stored credential — insight not violated here.

Core-dump/checkpoint write was the entire purpose of the unit. The checkpoint was written successfully (after the Read pivot). No reconstruction hallucination observed — insight not violated but relevant context.

recommendations: optimistic_write_without_read: This pattern appears structurally in every session that writes checkpoint JSON. A pre-write Read is the minimal fix and the write tool itself enforces this contract — the failure is a reminder, not a blocker. Recommend adding a Read call before any Write to a file that existed in a prior session rather than just continuing.

ask_user_question_instead_of_inputs_mcp: New pattern. CLAUDE.md §MCP tool preferences marks this MANDATORY. For workspace selection, service picker, or any structured multiple-choice input in future units, prefer mcp__inputs__pick_one over AskUserQuestion.

hook_bypass_via_absolute_grep_path: Positive signal — e7c8f9ae has zero absolute-grep calls. Do not recommend any change; monitor for regression in next measured session before classifying as resolved.

shell_tool_preference_violations: Rate dropping (25% new session vs 62% historical bc2715fa). lsof is a legitimate shell-only operation (no dedicated tool). wc-l and head via Bash are the remaining avoidable violations — prefer Glob for file counts and Read with limit for head equivalents. No new hook can prevent this; internalization remains the only path.

re_edit_thrash + optimistic_edit_without_read: Plateau at low rates in new session (1 unit each vs 3 historically). Pattern appears when editing files with complex inline string matching requirements. No new recommendation beyond the existing read-before-edit discipline — current trajectory is acceptable.
